name: lifespan
description: David Sinclair's groundbreaking book about the science of aging — why we age, how we might slow or reverse the process, and what it means for the future of human health. Drawing on decades of research, Sinclair presents a hopeful vision of longer, healthier lives.
useCase: Understand the cutting-edge science of aging, what causes us to age, and what interventions may extend both lifespan and healthspan.
knownRisks: Some of Sinclair's claims about reversing aging in humans remain unproven; the science is still developing.
The book promotes specific supplements (NMN, resveratrol) that may not be effective for everyone.
Sinclair has financial interests in some of the companies developing longevity treatments.
